prompt: Create a vertical watercolor travel poster of Taipei in a refined hand-drawn urban sketch style, showing a busy downtown street scene with Taipei 101 centered in the distant background, slightly misty and blue-gray, framed by mid-rise buildings, shopfronts, Chinese signage, traffic lights, taxis, scooters, and many pedestrians crossing a broad intersection in the foreground. Use the original-looking street composition of a lively East Asian shopping district: a green awning storefront on the lower left, a tall orange vertical shop sign on the right reading “DING TAI FUNG”, striped red-and-white awning and warm shop windows on the right, leafy tree branches entering from the upper right, and layered signs and building facades receding toward the tower. Render with soft ink outlines, loose architectural linework, natural watercolor washes, subtle paint blooms, splatters, paper grain, and a delicate travel-journal atmosphere. Add poster typography in the upper left: large spaced serif title “TAIPEI”, handwritten subtitle “Everyday Urban Rhythm”, and a small three-line caption reading “Local moments, local flavors, and the rhythm of everyday life.” Include small red stamp-like marks near the left text. Keep the palette warm off-white paper, muted beige buildings, gray-blue shadows, soft greens, red-orange signage, and tiny dark figures. Make the image feel like a collectible watercolor city souvenir poster, detailed but airy, with no photo-realism, no hard digital edges, and no extra large text beyond the visible poster lettering.
